description_long: This object consists of three metal open-ended wrenches, each with a distinct patina and signs of heavy use. The wrenches are arranged upright, leaning against each other to form a tripod-like structure. The metal surfaces are darkened and mottled, with visible wear, scratches, and some areas of oxidation, suggesting long-term exposure to harsh environments. The open jaws at each end are of varying sizes, designed for gripping and turning nuts or bolts. The handles are flat and slightly tapered, with no visible grip pattern or rubberized sections. The overall appearance is utilitarian and robust, typical of hand tools found in urban garages, workshops, or roadside repair kits.
description: A group of three heavily used, open-ended metal wrenches with dark, worn surfaces and visible signs of age. The wrenches are arranged upright, showing their utilitarian design and robust construction.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open jaws and flat handles of all three wrenches, highlighting their worn surfaces and the tripod-like arrangement.
description_view_1: Side view emphasizing the thickness of the wrenches and the way they lean against each other, with the edges and profiles of the handles visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the opposite sides of the wrench heads and handles, with surface wear and some faint markings visible.
description_view_3: Oblique side view showing the overlapping arrangement and the varying widths of the wrench handles.
attribute_rust_or_wear_marks: Extensive wear and oxidation visible across all surfaces, especially on the jaws and handles; moderate rust patches present.
